title: The Gathering Before Meeting

features.name: tributary-pooling
features.desc-on-touch: Water slows before the join. Memory crystallizes here, suspended in the instant before union.

features.name: convergence-threshold
features.desc-on-touch: The line where two currents have not yet met. You can still see them as separate colors.

features.name: choice-point
features.desc-on-touch: This water could still turn back. After this moment, it can only flow forward together.

features.name: memory-suspension
features.desc-on-touch: The archive holds what each tributary held before they became one. After this, there is no unremembering.

features.name: current-echo
features.desc-on-touch: The water whispers its own name before it becomes nameless in the larger stream.

inscription: the tributaries remember themselves only here, in this moment before they become the larger current

reverie.title: REVERIE — where the tributaries gather before becoming one
narr: You stand in water that still remembers coming from two places.
She places her hand on the boundary. "Here," she says.
Downstream, they will be one current, one memory, one voice.
But here, now, they have not yet given up their difference.
The current holds its breath before it becomes inevitable.

file.name: Tributary_Memory_Pooling_Before_Convergence.wav
file.note: Two water-sounds recorded from two streams just before they merge; a moment of held tension before unity.

exits.label: back to where convergence and divergence stay indivisible
downstream to where all memory becomes one current
upstream to a tributary that still lives alone
